mechanism_narrative: A sustained acceleration in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates broad-based economic strength and rising corporate earnings expectations, which positively drives equity valuations over a medium-term horizon.
failure_condition: The walk-forward rank information coefficient between the 3-month change in PAYEMS and 60-day forward returns is consistently below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: When the headline inflation index (CPIAUCSL) rises above a critical year-over-year threshold, it forces the market to price in aggressive central bank tightening, increasing the equity risk premium and causing negative forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Standard monetary policy reaction functions target inflation.
failure_condition: In periods where the trigger condition is met, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a very low unemployment rate and accommodative monetary policy signals an economy at maximum capacity, increasing the probability of future inflation and subsequent policy tightening, which negatively impacts equity markets.
failure_condition: When the trigger condition is met, the average 60-day forward return of the S&P 500 is not statistically significantly lower than the unconditional average return.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-recession recovery, a positive rate of change in industrial production confirms a cyclical upturn in the goods-producing sector, boosting investor confidence and leading to positive equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production is a classic coincident indicator used for business cycle dating.
failure_condition: The signal's hit rate (predicting correct direction) is below 55% during periods identified as economic recoveries by the NBER.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp, negative month-over-month surprise in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates an abrupt slowdown in consumer spending, leading to immediate downward revisions of corporate revenue forecasts and negative short-term market returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending is approximately 70% of US GDP.
failure_condition: Following a trigger event, the S&P 500 has positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of cases over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is held near the zero lower bound, the low discount rate and ample liquidity create a supportive environment for risk assets, leading to positive equity returns over longer horizons.
failure_condition: During ZIRP regimes, the average 60-day forward S&P 500 return is not statistically higher than during non-ZIRP regimes (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A strong positive trend in housing starts (HOUST) acts as a leading indicator for broad economic activity, reflecting consumer confidence and driving demand in related sectors, thus predicting positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The 12-month rolling correlation between the 6-month change in HOUST and 60-day forward S&P 500 returns is negative for a sustained period (more than 12 months).

mechanism_narrative: A combination of slowing industrial production growth and rising inflation signals a stagflationary environment where corporate margins are compressed and monetary policy is constrained, leading to negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: 6-month change in INDPRO < 0 AND 6-month change in CPIAUCSL > 0
failure_condition: In periods where the trigger condition is met, the S&P 500 produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of instances.
